hero.title:
Software Won't Save
Your Business.
hero.subtitle: Unless you apply it right. We start with your problem — often, you don't need software at all.

reality.title: Most Businesses Don't Fail Because of Lack of Technology.
points: They fail because of unclear processes, misplaced priorities, or trying to fix things with tools they don't need. AI won't save a broken workflow. A new app won't fix a team that doesn't know what to prioritize.
Before you invest in digitalization or AI — ask yourself: is the problem really technology? Or is it that nobody wrote down how your business actually works?
Technology is a multiplier. It amplifies what already works. If nothing works yet — no tool will change that.

when.title: Technology Is a Multiplier, Not a Magic Wand.
when.intro: It doesn't create value from nothing. It makes what already works — faster, cheaper, more reliable. Here's when it actually helps:
items: You have a working process but it's full of repetitive manual work — automate just that part.
Don't automate what you haven't figured out yet. Fix the process first.

services.title: Two Ways We Can Help.

pathA.label: Running a business
pathA.title: Your business works — make it faster.
pathA.description: You already have customers, revenue, and a team. But things are slow, manual, or hard to scale. We find the bottlenecks and apply DX/AX where it actually saves time and money. No overengineering — only what moves the needle.

pathB.label: Launching something new
pathB.title: Test your idea with minimum engineering.
pathB.description: You have a service or product idea but don't know how to build it. We help you create a minimum sellable product — the smallest thing that proves your market works. Fast, lean, no waste.

services.difference: We ask more business questions than any dev agency you've met. Because the right answer is sometimes: don't build that.

hero.title: Still Running Your Business on Excel and Email?
hero.description: We build custom software that fits how your team actually works — not the other way around.

pain.title: Every Growing Business Hits This Wall

excel.title: Excel Is Your ERP
excel.description: Your most critical business data lives in spreadsheets that only one person understands.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Lives in Heads
knowledge.description: When a key employee is sick, entire processes stop.

offtheshelf.title: Off-the-Shelf Doesn't Fit
offtheshelf.description: You tried Salesforce or SAP but it's too complex, too expensive, or doesn't match your workflow.

start.title: Don't Know Where to Start
start.description: You know you need to digitize, but you don't know what to ask for or who to trust.

founder.title: I've Been Where You Are
founder.intro: I'm Johnny Koo, founder of KooStory. I know what it's like to build a business from scratch and hit the wall where spreadsheets stop working.

startup.title: Scaled a Startup 0 to 270 Employees
startup.description: Built a Korean tech company to ~€25M revenue — I've lived the growing pains of a scaling business firsthand.

bootcamp.title: Trained 1,000+ Developers
bootcamp.description: Founded a coding bootcamp — I know how to explain tech in simple words, without jargon.

requirements.title: I Build Requirements for You
requirements.description: My specialty: working with clients who don't know what to ask for. I translate your daily work into clear software specs.

berlin.title: Now in Berlin, Building for Germany
berlin.description: Bringing Korea's world-leading DX expertise to German Mittelstand companies.

workshop.title: Free Problem Discovery (60 min)
workshop.description: You describe your daily work. We diagnose what's costing you time and money.

document.title: You Get a Problem Discovery Report
document.description: A clear report with scores, bottleneck analysis, and a prioritized roadmap. It's yours — work on it yourself, hand it to another team, or let us take care of it.

build.title: You Solve It — or We Do
build.description: Many clients solve it themselves with our report and suggestions. If you need hands-on help, we're here. Minimum engineering, scalable technology, no overbuilding.

ai.title: AI Makes It Fast. We Make It Right.
ai.description: Yes, anyone can generate an app with AI in an afternoon. For a weekend project, that's great. But when your whole team depends on software every day — real customer data, real contracts, real money — you need more than a clever prompt.

architecture.title: Architecture, Not Just Code
architecture.description: AI generates functions. We design systems that scale with your business, integrate with your tools, and evolve as requirements change.

tested.title: Tested, Not Just Generated
tested.description: Every feature goes through automated tests, security reviews, and real-user validation before it touches your production environment.

owned.title: Owned, Not Rented
owned.description: Your codebase is clean, documented, and maintainable. No black boxes. Your next developer can read it, understand it, and extend it.

compliant.title: Compliant, Not Careless
compliant.description: GDPR, data residency, access controls, audit trails — the unsexy engineering that keeps your business legal and your customers' data safe.

ax.title: Want to Use AI — But Don't Know Where to Start?
ax.description: You've tried ChatGPT for a few tasks. But applying AI across your entire business workflow? That's a different challenge.

diagnosis.title: We Find Where AI Actually Helps
diagnosis.description: Not every process needs AI. We identify the high-impact areas where AI saves real time and money — not just the trendy ones.

workflow.title: End-to-End AI Workflows
workflow.description: Beyond one-off ChatGPT prompts. We design and implement AI-driven workflows that connect your tools, data, and team processes.

practical.title: Practical, Not Theoretical
practical.description: No 50-page AI strategy deck. You get working automations — document processing, email triage, data extraction, report generation — deployed in days.

independent.title: You Keep Running It
independent.description: We build it so your team can operate and evolve it. Clear documentation, simple interfaces, and AI tools your people actually understand.

ax.cta: Get a free AX assessment in your DX diagnosis

korea.title: Built with Korean Engineering. Made for German Business.
korea.description: South Korea is the world's #1 digitized country. We bring that speed and digital-first thinking to your Mittelstand company.

checklist.title: Before You Invest in AI or Digitalization
checklist.intro: Honest advice before you spend money. Most businesses don't need what they think they need.

step1.title: You probably don't need AI at all.
step1.description: Most AI adoption is FOMO, not business need. Unless AI is solving a specific, daily pain point, it's just an expensive experiment. ChatGPT is great — but using it for random tasks isn't "AI transformation."

step2.title: Most problems don't need custom software either.
step2.description: A well-structured spreadsheet, a clearer process, or a better template solves 80% of business problems. Digitizing a messy process just automates the mess. Fix the process first.

step3.title: Technology only helps when applied to the right problem.
step3.description: Overengineering wastes time and money. The goal isn't "more technology" — it's less friction, fewer errors, faster results. Only build what actually moves the needle.

step4.title: That's why we start with your business, not your tech stack.
step4.description: Unlike typical dev agencies, we spend most of our time asking about your processes, bottlenecks, and daily operations. We figure out what to build — or what not to build — before writing a single line of code.

step5.title: When we do build — it actually contributes.
step5.description: Customized DX/AX engineering that delivers measurable efficiency. Automation your team actually uses. AI workflows that save real hours, not theoretical ones. No bloat, no waste — only what matters.

workshop.title: Your CTO for an Hour — Free.
workshop.description: If your company had a CTO or tech team leader, they'd do this: sit down with you, understand your operations, and tell you what technology can and can't do for you. We do exactly that — for free.
workshop.how: 60% we listen and ask. 40% we tell you what's possible.
workshop.focus: We can't cover everything in 60 minutes. So we'll ask you: what's the most urgent problem right now? We focus on that — and go deep.

deliverables.roadmap: Prioritized roadmap — what to do first

workshop.ownership: The report is yours. Work on it yourself, hand it to a freelancer, or let us continue.
workshop.trust: No commitment. No sales pitch. Just honest answers.

dxReport.title: Problem Discovery Report — Shared Office
dxReport.subtitle: Real case: virtual address service at a shared office in Seoul.
dxReport.disclaimer: Sample from a real project. Your report will be tailored to your business.

emailDialog.title: View the full report
emailDialog.description: Enter your email to see the complete sample report.
emailDialog.consent: I agree that KooStory may store my email and contact me about their services. I can withdraw consent at any time.

summary.title: Executive Summary
summary.text: The virtual address service at this shared office in Seoul runs almost entirely on manual processes. Every customer touchpoint — inquiry, contract, payment — depends on a single contact person. This creates delays, human errors, and customer churn. Three high-impact automation opportunities were identified.

report.asIsIntro: We mapped the complete customer journey from first inquiry to monthly payment. Red markers show where delays and errors occur.

painPoints.title: Customer Pain Points
painPoints.intro: These are the moments where customers experience friction, delay, or frustration.

items.title: No response confirmation after inquiry
items.description: Customer submits a form and sees nothing — no confirmation email, no expected response time. They don't know if anyone received their inquiry.

items.title: Slow response time (hours to days)
items.description: Response depends on one person's availability. During busy periods, inquiries wait in inbox for 1-3 days. Competitors respond faster.

items.title: No self-service for payment management
items.description: To cancel, modify, or check payment status, customers must contact support via messenger. No way to manage their own subscription.

report.suggestionsIntro: Prioritized by customer impact. Each suggestion can be implemented independently.

priority.title: Priority Matrix
priority.intro: Suggested implementation order based on impact vs. effort.

nextSteps.title: Recommended Next Steps
items: Review this report and decide which suggestions align with your priorities.
You can implement these yourself, hand this to a developer or freelancer, or contact us for help.
If you'd like us to continue, we'll provide a detailed scope and timeline — no commitment until you're ready.

reportCta.title: Want a report like this for your business?
reportCta.description: Book a free 60-minute problem discovery session. You'll get a report tailored to your operations.

case.title: Virtual Address Service — Customer Journey Analysis

highlight1.title: Inquiry reply depends on one person
highlight1.description: Customer submits inquiry on landing page. Contact person receives email and has to reply manually. If they're busy or forget — the customer waits. Or leaves.
highlight1.impact: Customer complaint: slow response, no confirmation

highlight2.title: Automate inquiry response + contract flow
highlight2.description: Customer gets instant confirmation with pricing and contract link. No human bottleneck at the first touchpoint.
highlight2.value: No more waiting. No lost inquiries.

asIs.title: As-Is: Current Process
steps: Customer visits landing page, submits inquiry form (price, contract info)
Contact person manually replies with pricing and contract details
Customer agrees → staff manually sends e-signature contract
Customer pays via bank transfer → staff checks manually each month
problems: If busy or absent — inquiry sits in inbox. No auto-reply, no confirmation.
Delayed response (hours to days). Customer may contact competitors.
Forgot to send? Customer waits. Human error = lost deals.
Late payments, missing transfers, manual reconciliation every month.

items.detail: Customer gets instant confirmation with pricing and contract link.
items.value: No more waiting. No lost inquiries.
items.diy: DIY option: Use a form tool like Tally or Typeform with auto-reply. Works well for most cases, but limited customization.
items.pro: Full solution: Custom-built flow integrated with your existing system. No platform limits, fully branded, scalable.

items.detail: Contract is sent automatically when customer confirms.
items.diy: DIY option: Use DocuSign or PandaDoc with manual triggers. Covers basic needs, but manual steps remain.
items.pro: Full solution: API-integrated auto-send on customer confirmation. Zero human steps, zero forgotten contracts.

items.detail: Customer can pay, cancel, modify, or request refund on their own.
items.value: Full control over their subscription. No need to contact support.
items.diy: DIY option: Use Stripe Checkout or PayPal subscriptions. Quick to set up, but limited to their UI and workflows.
items.pro: Full solution: Custom payment system integrated with your operations. Full self-service portal, automated reconciliation.

contact.title: Get in Touch
contact.description: Have questions? Want to discuss your project? Drop us a message.

form.name: Name
form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us about your business challenge...

seo.title: Ilmo Koo — What I Do
seo.description: Real examples of business problems I have solved. Requirements workshops, process automation, custom software. Founder of Kooslab (Seoul) and KooStory (Berlin).

hero.title: Here's what I do — with real examples.
hero.subtitle: I'm Ilmo Koo, founder of Kooslab (Seoul) and KooStory (Berlin). When a partner asks "what can you actually do?", I'd rather show than tell.

cases.title: Real Problems I've Solved
cases.intro: Every project below started with a business problem, not a feature request. Here's what the customer was struggling with, what I did, and what changed.

groupA.title: I defined it — so someone else could build it right
groupA.intro: Requirements consulting projects where I created the requirements document, and the client used it to successfully outsource to a dev agency.

groupB.title: I built it
groupB.intro: End-to-end projects where I defined AND built the solution.

safety.label: Software Requirements
safety.pain: Had deep domain expertise in safety solutions but no idea how to turn it into a software product. Didn't know what to ask for or how to brief developers.
safety.did: Ran requirements workshops, wrote detailed user stories, created wireframes and process maps. Discovered features and pages the client hadn't even considered.
safety.result: Result: Client got a clear development roadmap and requirements document. Successfully outsourced development to an agency with zero confusion.

onui.label: CRM Software Requirements
onui.pain: Needed a custom CRM but couldn't articulate what they needed. Previous attempts with dev agencies failed because requirements were unclear.
onui.did: Consulted on the full CRM requirements — workflows, data models, user stories. Created a comprehensive requirements document.
onui.result: Result: Successfully outsourced to a dev agency with a clear brief. No back-and-forth, no scope creep.

mindlogic.label: SSO Integration Requirements
mindlogic.pain: Their AI product (FactChat, similar to ChatGPT) needed to integrate with each university's own authentication system (SSO). Every university had a different login system.
mindlogic.did: Created an improved client requirements questionnaire that standardized the information gathering across universities.
mindlogic.result: Result: Made the SSO integration project 50% faster by eliminating back-and-forth communication. One questionnaire, clear answers, straight to implementation.

finnest.label: Finance Automation
finnest.pain: 100% of financial data lived in Excel spreadsheets with complicated formulas. People edited and broke formulas. Hard to operate, hard to scale. Getting data from banks and card companies was manual.
finnest.did: Built automated data extraction/scraping that runs every 24 hours, pulling data from banks and card systems. Integrated the extracted data into live operational spreadsheets with their existing complex formulas. Used n8n for workflow automation.
finnest.result: Result: Eliminated daily manual data entry. Financial operations run automatically with reliable data.

workspace.label: Shared Office Operations
workspace.pain: Shared office in Seoul managed all contracts and payments the traditional way — manual spreadsheet work for everything.
workspace.did: Built custom subscription payment and contract management software with e-signature for online contract signing.
workspace.result: Result: 90% of manual operations eliminated. Contracts, billing, and payments now automated.

grocery.label: Delivery Kiosk
grocery.pain: At a major grocery department in Seoul, customers wrote their delivery addresses by hand. Slow process (60–100 seconds per order), required a dedicated staff member.
grocery.did: Built a touch-based kiosk connected to a logistics API with a favorite address feature. Customers enter their mobile number, load their saved address, and complete the order.
grocery.result: Result: Order time dropped from 60–100 seconds to 10 seconds. Eliminated one full-time hire — customers now self-serve.

tour.label: Tour Booking Platform
tour.pain: Tour guides in Berlin and across Europe managed private tours completely by hand — bookings, payments, communication, everything manual.
tour.did: Built a platform where tour guides upload their tour products online. Travelers can browse, pay, and reserve online, with automatic messenger notifications.
tour.result: Result: Tour guides went from manual operations to an automated booking and payment system.

parking.label: LPR Integration
parking.pain: Client wanted a CCTV-based AI parking management system but needed License Plate Recognition integrated into their existing cloud-based parking software.
parking.did: Integrated a License Plate Recognition (LPR) module into their cloud-based parking management software.
parking.result: Result: Automated vehicle identification and parking management through CCTV.

howIWork.title: How I Work
howIWork.p1: Partner, not vendor. I stay until it works for your business. If something needs to change after delivery, we change it.
howIWork.p2: Flexible and lightweight. I act as your tech team. No big agency overhead, no months of onboarding.
howIWork.p3: I'd rather build one thing you use daily than ten features that collect dust. If a feature turns out to be useless, we cut it. Your success is my metric.

about.title: About Me
about.p1: Engineer by training — aerospace engineering at the University of Maryland. Requirements and specs since day one: Korean Air (flight control systems), Vector Korea (automotive embedded software). Scaled Code States to 200+ team, $27M revenue as CPO — automated everything because growth demanded it.
about.p3: Seoul + Berlin. Korean native, English business-level, German in progress.

wontDo.title: What I Won't Do
wontDo.p1: I politely decline building software nobody will use.
wontDo.p2: If your idea doesn't solve a real daily problem, I'll tell you honestly.
wontDo.p3: Pretty code that creates no business value is waste.
wontDo.p4: Sometimes the best advice is "don't build that."

workshops.title: Workshops
workshops.intro: Whether you need clarity on what to build, or want to learn to build it yourself.

free.title: Problem Discovery Session
free.description: 60 min, online or offline. We diagnose your current workflows and you walk away with a DX diagnosis report. No commitment.

requirements.title: 1-Day Requirements Workshop
requirements.description: Full day, deep dive. You get a complete requirements document — user stories, process maps, priorities. Hand it to any dev team.

vibe.title: 1-Day Vibe Coding Workshop
vibe.description: Hands-on 1:1. I sit next to you (Berlin offline, otherwise online) and you build your own MVP or prototype by the end of the day.

paidLink.title: Paid workshops — early bird pricing available
paidLink.description: Requirements Workshop (200 EUR) and Vibe Coding Workshop (100 EUR). 50% off until April 30, 2026.

workshops.priceNote: Prices will rise soon. Book now to lock in the current rate.

seo.title: Workshops — KooStory
seo.description: 1-day workshops for business owners. Requirements workshop or vibe coding workshop. Early bird pricing until April 30, 2026.

hero.title: Workshops for Business Owners
hero.subtitle: Whether you need someone to define what to build, or you want to learn to build it yourself. One day, clear results.

requirements.title: Requirements Workshop
requirements.description: A full day dedicated to your business problem. You walk away with a complete requirements document that any development team can work from.

vibe.title: Vibe Coding Workshop
vibe.description: You build your own MVP or prototype by end of day. No coding experience needed — if you can use a computer, you can do this.
includes: Set up your AI coding environment
Learn to iterate and improve on your own
Your own deployed application by end of day
vibe.format: Berlin (I sit right next to you) or online

free.title: Free Problem Discovery
free.description: 60-minute session, online or offline. We diagnose your current workflows and deliver a DX report with actionable insights. No strings attached.

workshops.priceNote: Early bird pricing ends April 30, 2026. After that, regular prices apply (400 EUR / 200 EUR).

items.a: No. The requirements workshop is designed for business owners who know their problem but not the technical solution. The vibe coding workshop teaches you from zero — if you can use email, you can do this.

items.q: What if I'm not in Berlin?
items.a: Both workshops work perfectly online. For the vibe coding workshop, I share my screen and guide you step by step.

items.q: What do I get at the end?
items.a: Requirements workshop: a complete document you can hand to any developer. Vibe coding workshop: a working application you built yourself, deployed and accessible.
Yes — and it's a great combination. Define what to build first, then build the MVP yourself. Contact me for a combined rate.
